commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Manual maintainer handling is needed because this branch requires product/security approval and contributor live proof beyond the concrete code repairs.; Needs attention: The diff intentionally changes network authorization and public ingress behavior, so it needs maintainer security review before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Send pond in coordinator lease requests — internal/cli/coordinator.go:602-645 - [P1] Persis... (sha=ba11d66da18556d3f1f86d993cd6c38748ccf5ec)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a preview `pond` feature with `--pond` and `--expose`, provider capability-based peer discovery, Tailscale ACL/hosts bootstrap, URL bridge adapters, SSH-mesh connect, Worker label support, docs, and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR blockers: source inspection shows the coordinator request lacks `pond`, generic claims do not persist cfg.Pond, `--export` returns before tunnels start, and `release --pond` is documented without a matching stop/release flag.
commands.review_followup: Manual maintainer handling is needed because this branch requires product/security approval and contributor live proof beyond the concrete code repairs.

target.title: feat: introduce pond (peer discovery + bridge + ssh-mesh)
target.body:
## Motivation — when and why

Every team that owns more than one service eventually hits the same wall: their integration tests need more than one box. Today that lives as `docker-compose` + tunneling scripts — and compose's loopback hides the bugs that actually matter at this seam: saga / two-phase ordering, mutual TLS, cookie / CORS / redirect paths across origins, retry backoff under real RTT, partition behavior, GraphQL federation timing. The "GPU" container in the same compose file is a CPU-only sham. Spinning up a shared staging env doesn't help — it serializes everyone behind one Slack thread.

`--pond NAME` is the smallest primitive that unblocks this: a label that groups N leases into a private network (Tailscale on managed Linux, HTTPS bridge on URL providers, operator-side `ssh -L` everywhere else), addressable by role-name from the test driver, that lives and dies as one unit. Single-box use is a "pond of one" — existing flows unchanged.

### When to reach for a pond

- **Integration tests that need >1 box** — API on cheap CPU, ML inference on a real GPU, DB on a third lease; reach each other as `ml.cbx`, `db.cbx`. The headline use case.
- **Per-PR isolated E2E env** — every PR gets its own staging; pond dies with the PR. Replaces shared-staging Slack contention.
- **Multi-repo end-to-end** — a change in `auth` tested against `gateway` / `billing` / `reports`, each on its own box.
- **Per-PR build farm** — 30 helper boxes on Islo for Bazel/C++ acceleration; forensically clean, die with the PR.
- **Distributed training validation** — 8-node DDP across Hetzner + RunPod with `torchrun --master_addr=rank-0.cbx`.
- **Cross-vendor cost arbitrage** — cheap CPU on Hetzner, on-demand GPU on Modal, all in one pond.

### Why "any one provider" doesn't work

- **Hardware spread you can't get from one vendor** (macOS arm64 + macOS x86_64 + Linux arm64 + Linux x86_64 + Windows; no provider has all five).
- **GPU quota walls** — multi-week tickets on hyperscalers, seconds on Modal.
- **Cost arbitrage at the leg level** — bare-metal CPU on Hetzner is 3–5× cheaper than hyperscaler list price.
- **Failover** — when one provider has a bad day, re-lease the affected slug against another in the same pond; blast radius is one vendor, not the whole afternoon.
- **Geographic / residency** — real EU↔US↔APAC latency triangles; GDPR/LGPD per-slug pinning.

### When *not* to use it

- **Tightly-coupled HPC** (MPI / NCCL) across providers — the public-internet latency floor is too high. Keep tightly-coupled jobs on one provider + region.
- **Untrusted multi-tenant** — default-allow within a pond. For agent-isolation cases see `--isolation per-slug` (future).
- **macOS / Windows peer reachability** — documented gap.

## Summary
- Adds `--pond NAME` flag + reserved `pond=` provider label. Single-box use is a pond of one; existing flows unchanged.
- Three transport planes, derived from each provider's `Spec().Features` via a new `ProviderCapabilities` model:
  - **Tailscale** (`FeatureTailscale`) — managed Linux (Hetzner / Azure / GCP) peer mesh via ACL tag + cloud-init systemd timer rewriting `/etc/hosts.cbx`. Stable hostnames `<slug>.cbx`.
  - **Bridge** (`FeatureURLBridge`) — delegated URL providers (Islo / E2B / Railway live; Modal / Cloudflare / Tensorlake stubs) expose HTTPS endpoints via the new `BridgeProvider` interface.
  - **SSH-mesh** (`FeatureSSH`) — **any** SSH-capable provider gets operator-side `ssh -L` via `crabbox pond connect [--export]`. Hetzner / Azure / GCP / AWS / Proxmox / static SSH / RunPod / exe-dev / Daytona / Sprites / Namespace / Semaphore / local-container / Parallels are all eligible. Tailscale stays the preferred plane when both are available.
- `BridgePeer.transports` reports the *full* set of planes per member (`["tailnet","ssh"]` for Hetzner, `["url"]` for Islo, etc.); legacy `transport` field still reports the primary recommended one (backward compat).
- `crabbox pond connect NAME` is cross-provider — drops the single-`--provider` requirement (it's now a filter), iterates every pond member whose provider declares `FeatureSSH`.
- `crabbox doctor --pond NAME` reports honest cross-plane reachability.
- Worker reserves the `pond` label key. Broker never holds Tailscale credentials.
- Folds in the bridge plane (#136) and SSH-mesh (#137) into one PR.

## Naming
Renamed `crew` → `pond` to align with the crab-biology naming already used in the org (crabbox, crabyard, crabpot, Clawd). `pool` was the first instinct but `internal/cli/pool.go` already owns it (warm-lease pool).

## Verification
- `go test -race ./internal/cli/...`
- `go vet ./...`
- `cd worker && pnpm test && pnpm typecheck`

## Live smoke — Bridge plane (Islo)
```
$ crabbox warmup --pond ps001 --slug w --provider islo --islo-memory-mb 2048 --islo-disk-gb 10 --ttl 5m
leased isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e slug=w provider=islo sandbox=crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
warmup complete total=42.17s

$ crabbox pond peers --pond ps001 --json
{"members":[{"slug":"w","leaseID":"isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e","provider":"islo","pond":"ps001","transport":"url","transports":["url"],"endpoint":""}]}

$ crabbox release --provider islo isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
released lease=isb_crabbox-crabbox-94c14e sandbox=crabbox-crabbox-94c14e
```

## Live smoke — Tailscale plane (ACL bootstrap end-to-end)
Against a real tailnet with `TS_API_KEY` exported. Read the live policy (HuJSON decode — R1 fix), merged the new row into `tagOwners` + `grants`, PUT back with `If-Match`. New tag's owner segment is `ipdkyao` — `base32(sha256("yossi-eliaz"))[:7]`, the R4 fix — sitting beside the stale `cbx-crew-yossi-e-alpha` row from earlier truncation-era testing, confirming the hash doesn't collide. Idempotent re-run of `crabbox doctor --pond live-smoke --provider hetzner` reports **`ok pond: Tailscale plane auto-managed`**. Test row cleaned up after capture.

## Live smoke — SSH-mesh plane cross-provider (local-container / Docker)
Proves the capability refactor's core claim: a non-SSH-only provider (`local-container`, declares `FeatureSSH` but not in the legacy SSH-only class) is now `pond connect`-eligible.
```
$ crabbox warmup --provider local-container --pond local-smoke --slug web --expose 8080 --ttl 5m
provisioned lease=cbx_3960ccf8b93a container=aeb808878095 state=ready
leased cbx_3960ccf8b93a slug=web provider=local-container ip=127.0.0.1
ready ssh=crabbox@127.0.0.1:61988 network=public

$ cat ~/Library/Application\ Support/crabbox/state/claims/cbx_3960ccf8b93a.json
{ "leaseID":"cbx_3960ccf8b93a", "slug":"web", "provider":"local-container",
  "pond":"local-smoke", "repoRoot":"/Users/zozo123/crabbox", ... }

$ crabbox pond peers --pond local-smoke --json
{ "members": [
    { "slug":"web", "leaseID":"cbx_3960ccf8b93a", "provider":"local-container",
      "pond":"local-smoke", "transport":"pending", "transports":["ssh"],
      "endpoint":"", "note":"ssh endpoint not yet recorded for this lease" } ] }

$ crabbox pond connect local-smoke --export
pond "local-smoke" SSH-mesh ready (1 forwards)
export CRABBOX_POND_WEB_8080=127.0.0.1:51820
wrote ~/.crabbox/pond/local-smoke/hosts
wrote ~/.crabbox/pond/local-smoke/env
  -L 127.0.0.1:51820 -> web:8080
```
Note: no `--provider` flag on `pond connect` — it iterates every SSH-mesh-capable provider in the pond by capability lookup, exactly the cross-provider eligibility the refactor unlocks.

## Codex review feedback resolved
- `pool.go` coordinator-path label drop → `CoordinatorLease.Pond` added + propagated in `leaseToServerTarget`.
- `pool.go` typed-slice filter → JSON round-trip in `filterJSONListViewByPond`.
- `doctor_pond.go` substring-match → per-rule structural acls/grants parse via `pondACLSelfPeerRule` / `pondGrantSelfPeerRule`.
- `lease_flags.go` bootstrap-on-reuse → guarded on `existingLeaseID == ""`.
- `pond_acl.go` hujson decode (handles `//` comments + trailing commas in real policies).
- Owner segment switched from 7-char left-truncate to stable `base32(sha256)` hash.
- `internal/providers/localcontainer/backend.go` claim sidecar now persists the `pond` field via `ClaimLeaseForRepoProviderPond` (was the generic helper without pond) so `pond peers` / `pond connect` see local-container members.

## Capability-model refactor (penultimate commit)
The original `providerTransportClass` switch hardcoded one transport per provider — that collapsed a 2-D space (provider × transport) into 1-D and *also* miscategorised Proxmox / static SSH as `TransportTailnet` despite neither declaring `FeatureTailscale`. The refactor replaces it with `ProviderCapabilities` derived from each provider's own `Spec().Features` set:
- Providers self-declare via `FeatureTailscale`, `FeatureSSH`, `FeatureURLBridge` — no central hardcoded table.
- `BridgePeer.transports` reports the full set; `transport` reports the recommended primary (Tailscale > URL > SSH).
- `pond connect` is cross-provider: every claim whose provider has `FeatureSSH` is eligible.
- Net behavior change: `crabbox pond connect mypond` now works against Hetzner / Azure / GCP / AWS out of the box; previously it would refuse because those providers' primary transport is `tailnet`.
- Test coverage: `TestCollectPondMembersAcrossProvidersFiltersByCapability`, `TestCollectPondMembersAcrossProvidersHonorsProviderFilter`, `TestProviderCapabilitiesPrimary`, `TestProviderCapabilitiesAvailable` (new).

## Deferred (follow-up PRs)
- Modal / Cloudflare / Tensorlake live URL adapters (currently advertise `FeatureURLBridge` but emit `BridgeState=unsupported` until the provider APIs expose per-sandbox ingress).
- macOS / Windows plane.
- `crabbox pond gc` for stale Tailscale tag cleanup.
- Pond cost aggregation.
- `--isolation per-slug` mode for agent-isolation use cases.
- Doctor matrix multi-transport awareness (currently uses Primary; honest enough for now).
- `--via {tailscale,ssh-mesh,url}` operator-override flag for explicit transport choice.

## API stability
`pond` is **preview** for v0.x. The reserved label key stays; flag shape may evolve. Stable from v1.0.

## History
10 focused commits on top of `main`, no merge commits:
```
fix(pond): persist pond label in local-container claim sidecar
test(pond): cross-provider eligibility + capability assertions
docs(pond): explain the capability model & cross-provider SSH-mesh
refactor(pond): capability-based transport selection across all providers
feat(pond): SSH-mesh plane — operator ssh -L tunnels via `pond connect`
fix(pond): Codex review + R1 hujson + R4 stable owner hash
docs(pond): replace verbose design doc with KISS usage-focused page
refactor(pond): rename crew → pond across CLI, Worker, docs
feat(crew): bridge plane — Islo / E2B / Railway URL adapters
feat(crew): foundation — Tailscale plane, ACL bootstrap, hosts timer
```
target.author: zozo123

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
